headline: Pahang Bauxite Mining Residue Contaminates 2,800 Hectares of Farmland
context: A 2025 UMP soil study found 2,800 hectares of agricultural land in Kuantan remain contaminated with heavy metals from bauxite mining activities that peaked in 2015.

cards.big: The 2016 bauxite moratorium was supposed to allow environmental recovery.
cards.sub: 9 years later, 2,800 hectares of farmland remain contaminated. No remediation programme exists.

cards.big: Aluminium and arsenic levels exceed safe agricultural limits at 340 sampling points.
cards.sub: Crops grown on contaminated soil show heavy metal uptake exceeding food safety standards.

cards.big: 1,200 farming families lost access to contaminated land.
cards.sub: Average income loss is RM1,800 per month. Government compensation totalled RM3,000 per family — a one-time payment.

cards.big: Mining royalties earned by Pahang from bauxite were RM320M between 2013 and 2016.
cards.sub: Estimated soil remediation cost for 2,800 hectares is RM480M — 50% more than total royalties earned.

cards.big: Pahang earned RM320M from bauxite and owes RM480M in cleanup. The state profited on paper and lost in reality.
Mining bonds that cover full remediation costs should be mandatory before extraction begins. The bauxite episode proves that post-facto cleanup is neither funded nor performed.
